methods.method_name: Field Experiment Setup for Fertilizer Study
methods.description: Describes the design and setup of a field experiment to study the effect of conventional and ultradispersed forms of sapropel, vermicompost, and diatomite as fertilizer supplements on barley growth, following the method of B.A. Dospekhova.

steps.description: The soil was gray forest medium loamy and had the following characteristics: humus -3.2 %, рНKCl. -6.23, Ng -1.03 meq per 100 g of soil, the sum of absorbed bases -39.6 meq per 100 g of soil, Nacid -98.0 mg/kg, P2O5 -184.0 mg/kg, K2O -106 mg/kg.
steps.searchable_summary: Document soil characteristics of the experimental site.
outputs: Documented soil characteristics (humus, pH, cation exchange capacity, N, P2O5, K2O levels)

steps.description: Barley of the "Prairia" variety was used in the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Select and specify crop variety for the experiment.

steps.description: The experiments used sapropel deposits of Lake Beloe RT, diatomite of the Inzensky deposit of the Ulyanovsk region, biohumus produced by "Green-PIK" (Kovrov, Vladimir region).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain experimental materials from specified sources.
inputs: Sources (Lake Beloe RT, Inzensky deposit, Green-PIK)

steps.description: in the form of aqueous suspensions.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare experimental materials as aqueous suspensions.

steps.description: The effect of fertilizers in conventional and ultradispersed forms was studied in a field experiment according to the method of B.A. Dospekhova [6].
steps.searchable_summary: Design the field experiment according to the method of B.A. Dospekhova [6].

steps.description: the placement of the plots was randomized.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement randomized placement of experimental plots.

steps.description: The experiment was repeated three times.
steps.searchable_summary: Set the number of experimental replicates to three.
